title: Insightly Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Insightly** is an SMB-focused CRM exposing a comprehensive REST API at `https://api.{pod}.insightly.com/v3.1`. This connector â€” `InsightlyConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "insightly"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Endpoint root | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Contacts | `/Contacts` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Organisations | `/Organisations` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Opportunities | `/Opportunities` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Leads | `/Leads` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Projects | `/Projects` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Tasks | `/Tasks` | list, get, create, update, delete |
| Events | `/Events` | list |
| Notes | `/Notes` | list |
| Emails | `/Emails` | list |
| Pipelines | `/Pipelines` | list |
| Users | `/Users` | list (health-check probes `/Users/Me`) |
| Custom Objects | `/CustomObjects` | list |
| Tags | `/Tags/{record_type}` | list |

The connector normalises Contacts (and the helpers normalise Organisations / Opportunities / Leads) into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC â€” all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::InsightlyHTTPClient`).

### Pod-aware base URL

Insightly hosts each tenant on a **regional pod** â€” `na1` (North America), `eu1` (Europe), `apac1` (Asia-Pacific), etc. The pod is part of every URL when you are signed in, and the connector builds the REST base URL from it:

```
https://api.{pod}.insightly.com/v3.1
```

Default pod is `na1`. Override via the `pod` install field, or supply a custom `base_url` for private/staging deployments.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate an Insightly API key

1. Sign in to https://crm.insightly.com.
2. Click your **profile avatar** (top-right) â†’ **User Settings**.
3. Scroll to the **API Key** section.
4. Click **Copy** next to the long alphanumeric key (format: `XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX`).
5. Note your pod from the URL (e.g. `https://crm.na1.insightly.com/...` â†’ pod is `na1`).

> **Important:** The free Insightly plan does not include API access. You must be on a paid plan (Plus, Professional, or Enterprise) or trial.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP â†’ Manage Connectors â†’ Insightly**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `pod` (text) | Region pod (`na1`, `eu1`, `apac1`, â€¦) |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.{pod}.insightly.com/v3.1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` + `pod` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `InsightlyHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /Users/Me` as the cheapest authenticated request. On 200 â†’ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 â†’ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 â†’ `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 â†’ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` (with a structured-log warning).

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Insightly REST API uses **HTTP Basic authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is the HTTP Basic **username**; the **password is empty**.
- Wire format: `Authorization: Basic base64(api_key + ":")`.
- This is the Insightly-specific gotcha â€” the trailing colon is mandatory, otherwise the server returns 401.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` + `pod`. Initialises `InsightlyHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Insightly endpoint. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key (surface compatibility with BaseConnector). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /Users/Me` â€” minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called â€” no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None â€” the api_key lives in `self.config` (encrypted by the gateway). |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

Note the trailing `:` inside the base64 â€” it represents the empty password.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` â€” never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | HTTP Basic username; password is empty |
| `pod` | text | yes (default `"na1"`) | `self.pod` | Region pod â€” drives the base URL template |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` | Full URL override; computed from pod when blank |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default `60`) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key", "pod"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_INSIGHTLY_BASE_TEMPLATE = "https://api.{pod}.insightly.com/v3.1"
```

`api_key` is **never** a class constant â€” it's per-tenant and read only from `self.config`.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `InsightlyConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::InsightlyHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key` + `pod`; initialise `InsightlyHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth â€” returns a TokenInfo whose access_token is the api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /Users/Me` â€” single-record authenticated probe. Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/Contacts?top=200&skip=â€¦`, normalises each Contact via `helpers.normalizer.normalize_contact`, and ingests via `ingest_document`. Stores last skip offset in metadata key `"last_skip"`.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(top=50, skip=0, brief=False) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /Contacts?top=&skip=&brief=`.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /Contacts/{id}`.

### `async create_contact(first_name?, last_name?, email?, phone?) -> Dict`
`POST /Contacts`. At least one of first_name/last_name/email is needed.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, fields: Dict) -> Dict`
`PUT /Contacts/{id}` â€” Insightly's PUT requires CONTACT_ID inside the body; the connector merges it in automatically.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /Contacts/{id}`. Idempotent â€” 404 â†’ `{deleted, already_missing: True}`.

children.title: Organisations
children.content:
### `async list_organisations(top=50, skip=0)`
`GET /Organisations`.

### `async get_organisation(organisation_id)`
`GET /Organisations/{id}`.

### `async create_organisation(organisation_name, phone?, website?)`
`POST /Organisations`. organisation_name is required.

### `async update_organisation(organisation_id, fields)`
`PUT /Organisations/{id}` â€” adds ORGANISATION_ID to the body.

### `async delete_organisation(organisation_id)`
`DELETE /Organisations/{id}`. Idempotent.

children.title: Opportunities + Leads
children.content:
### `async list_opportunities / get_opportunity / create_opportunity / update_opportunity / delete_opportunity`
`GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /Opportunities[/{id}]`. `create_opportunity` requires `opportunity_name` and a probability in `[0, 100]`.

### `async list_leads / get_lead / create_lead / update_lead / delete_lead`
`GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /Leads[/{id}]`.

children.title: Projects + Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_projects / get_project / create_project / update_project / delete_project`
`GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /Projects[/{id}]`. `create_project` requires `project_name`.

### `async list_tasks / get_task / create_task / update_task / delete_task`
`GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /Tasks[/{id}]`. `create_task` requires `title`.

children.title: Events Â· Notes Â· Emails Â· Pipelines Â· Users Â· Custom Objects Â· Tags
children.content:
### `async list_events(top=50, skip=0)`
`GET /Events`.

### `async list_notes(top=50, skip=0)`
`GET /Notes`.

### `async list_emails(top=50, skip=0)`
`GET /Emails`.

### `async list_pipelines()`
`GET /Pipelines`.

### `async list_users()`
`GET /Users`.

### `async list_custom_objects()`
`GET /CustomObjects`.

### `async list_tags(record_type="contacts")`
`GET /Tags/{record_type}` â€” record_type âˆˆ `contacts | organisations | opportunities | leads | projects | tasks`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
InsightlyError                        # base; carries status_code + response_body
â”œâ”€â”€ InsightlyAuthError                # 401 / 403
â”œâ”€â”€ InsightlyBadRequestError          # 400
â”œâ”€â”€ InsightlyNotFoundError            # 404
â”œâ”€â”€ InsightlyConflictError            # 409
â”œâ”€â”€ InsightlyRateLimitError           # 429 â€” retry_after_s
â””â”€â”€ InsightlyServerError              # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
InsightlyNotFound     = InsightlyNotFoundError
InsightlyNetworkError = InsightlyServerError    # legacy name from older code
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `InsightlyNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 â†’ ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   â€” logged as warning
5xx â†’ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   AUTHENTICATED) â€” via InsightlyNetworkError
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` or `pod` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Pod is the subdomain in your Insightly URL â€” `https://crm.na1.insightly.com/...` â†’ pod is `na1`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Insightly â€” API key plan does not include API access | Upgrade to a paid Insightly plan (free plan has no API). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 â€” API key revoked, wrong, or rotated | Re-copy from User Settings â†’ API Key. Note the trailing `:` in the Basic auth is part of the format, not your key. |
| 404 on EVERY endpoint | Wrong pod | The base URL must match your tenant's region. Check the URL while signed in and update the `pod` install field. |
| `insightly.http.retry` warnings spike | 429 from Insightly's per-minute cap | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` to 30 or batch fewer requests. |
| `Synced 0/0 contacts` | Sandbox or empty account | Verify in Insightly UI that contacts exist; check the API key has full account access (no scope filter). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'InsightlyNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed â€” `InsightlyNetworkError = InsightlyServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Network error on health_check from inside a corporate network | Outbound HTTPS to `api.{pod}.insightly.com` blocked | Allowlist the pod-specific hostname and port 443 in the egress policy. |
